claim: Specific Hand B high-frequency tokens (beyond chedy) show section-concentration patterns analogous to chedy's biological-section concentration. If three or more tokens show section-specific concentrations with different preferred sections, Hand B has content-linked vocabulary and section-specific markers — establishing a framework for token-to-content-type associations.
evidence_for: H-BV-HAND-B-SECTION-BREAKDOWN-01 confirmed chedy as biological-specific marker (d=+1.08, biological-sole driver for chedy section effect). The question is whether this is a one-off or a pattern.
Hand B has balanced section composition (32 herbal / 23 recipes / 19 biological / 5 text-only / 3 cosmological) with sufficient power to detect per-section markers for 20 tokens.

If Hand B text responds to content (as HAND-B-SECTION-INDEPENDENCE-01 REFUTED establishes), we would expect MULTIPLE tokens to show section-specific concentrations, not just chedy. The alternative is that chedy is a single outlier — which would weaken the text-content coupling interpretation.
The top-20 tokens (excluding chedy) cover tokens appearing 144-435 times total — all common enough to have statistical power per section.
evidence_against: Multiple-testing with 20 tokens × 5 sections = 100 tests requires Bonferroni correction. At alpha=0.01 family-wise, per-test threshold is p<0.0001. This is strict; some genuine but moderate concentrations may not reach it.

Tokens like 'y' (1-char), 'l' (1-char), 'ar', 'or', 'al', 'ol' may be EVA fragments rather than meaningful morphemes. If they correspond to glyph-level generative-process artefacts, they won't show content-correlation regardless of underlying interpretation.

Section assignment categorises 5 Hand B sections. text-only has only 5 folios and cosmological has 3 folios — statistical power at these thresholds is limited. Concentrations in these small sections may be detected as significant spuriously.

Even if multiple tokens show section-concentration, the interpretation is delicate: token X concentrating in section Y means X is distributionally more common in Y-folios, NOT that X means the topic of Y. Lexical hypothesis requires additional validation.
test:
LOCKED PROCEDURE.

  DATA: Hand B folios with >= 30 tokens from voynich-corpus.json. Same filter as H-BV-HAND-B-SECTION-INDEPENDENCE-01.

  TOP-20 TOKENS (LOCKED, excluding chedy):
    ol, shedy, aiin, daiin, qokeedy, ar, qokain, qokedy, or,
    qokeey, chey, qokaiin, shey, al, dar, y, okaiin, qokal,
    otedy, l

  SECTIONS (locked, with n >= 3 folios): herbal (32), recipes (23), biological (19), text-only (5), cosmological (3).

  METRIC: per-folio token frequency = count of token in folio / total tokens in folio.

  PER-TEST PROCEDURE (100 tests = 20 tokens x 5 sections):
    For each (token T, section S):
      xs = token T frequencies across folios IN section S
      ys = token T frequencies across folios NOT in section S
      Welch's t-test on xs vs ys, two-tailed.
      Cohen's d (standardised mean difference) of xs vs ys.
    Record: t-stat, p-value, Cohen's d, n_in, n_out, mean_in, mean_out.

  SIGNIFICANCE: Bonferroni-corrected p < 0.01/100 = 0.0001.

  CLASSIFICATION per token (across the 5 section tests):
    SECTION-CONCENTRATED: exactly one section has p < 0.0001 AND Cohen's d >= 0.5 (positive direction). Token is a candidate marker for that section.
    DISTRIBUTED: two or more sections have p < 0.0001 AND positive d. Token is elevated in multiple sections but not one specifically.
    UNIVERSAL: no section reaches p < 0.0001 AND max |d| < 0.3 across all sections. Token is used at similar rates everywhere.
    VARIABLE: no section reaches p < 0.0001 but max |d| >= 0.3. Token varies but not sharply enough.

  DECISION RULE (LOCKED):
    CONFIRMED_SYSTEMATIC: >= 3 tokens classified as SECTION-CONCENTRATED with AT LEAST TWO DIFFERENT sections as the preferred one. Hand B has distributed content-linked vocabulary.
    PARTIAL_PATTERN: 1-2 tokens classified as SECTION-CONCENTRATED. chedy's concentration is not unique but not widely shared.
    CHEDY_OUTLIER: 0 tokens classified as SECTION-CONCENTRATED. chedy is an outlier; the section effects are diversity-driven not lexeme-driven.

  SUPPLEMENTARY ANALYSIS (always reported):
    Full 20x5 table of (mean_in, mean_out, d, p) per (token, section) pair.
    Count per category (section-concentrated, distributed, universal, variable).
    For each section-concentrated token, report the preferred section and the effect size.

  WORKED EXAMPLE:
    Token 'ol': frequency per Hand B folio.
    In biological folios: [freq_f75v, freq_f76r, ...] — 19 values.
    In non-biological folios: 63 values.
    Welch t-test on ol-frequency across biological vs non-biological. Compute d.
    Repeat for (ol, recipes), (ol, herbal), (ol, text-only), (ol, cosmological).
    If any of the 5 tests passes p < 0.0001 AND has positive d > 0.5, ol is classified as section-concentrated for that section.
test_metric: Per-token classification into {section-concentrated, distributed, universal, variable}. Aggregate count of section-concentrated tokens.
test_threshold: CONFIRMED_SYSTEMATIC if >=3 section-concentrated with >=2 different sections. PARTIAL_PATTERN if 1-2. CHEDY_OUTLIER if 0.

notable_non_concentrated_patterns.note: d=-1.15 for biological — aiin is AVOIDED in biological (negative direction). Section-avoidance counterpart to concentration.
d=-1.30 for biological — ar is strongly depleted in biological. Consistent with biological being its own vocabulary regime.
d=+1.40 biological at p=0.0001 (boundary miss of strict <0.0001 threshold); d=-0.88 herbal at p<0.0001. Would classify as biological-concentrated under slightly looser threshold.

verdict_note: CONFIRMED_SYSTEMATIC. 8 of 20 top Hand B tokens (40%) show section-concentration, spread across 3 different sections (biological, herbal, recipes). Biological has 5 concentrated markers (plus chedy from prior test = 6 total), recipes has 2, herbal has 1. This is strong systematic evidence that Hand B vocabulary is content-coupled at the lexical level. Additionally, 12 of 20 tokens are VARIABLE (vary across sections but don't pass the strict threshold), with many showing section-AVOIDANCE (negative d in specific sections). ZERO tokens are universal. Every top-20 token shows some section variation. This pattern is incompatible with a 'generative-process overlay' that ignores content; it is consistent with 'text responding to content with section-specific vocabulary'.
findings_for_followup: CRITICAL FOR v2: Hand B text responds to content at the lexical level. Specific tokens are section-concentrated with large effect sizes (d = 0.73 to 2.09). The generative-process-overlay interpretation from earlier Brain-V work no longer fits Hand B — it is clearly content-coupled.

BIOLOGICAL VOCABULARY (6 tokens total, including chedy from prior test): shedy (d=2.09), ol (1.73), qokain (1.82), qokedy (1.19), shey (1.15), chedy (1.08). All concentrate in biological with large positive effects. Many share visual characters (sh-, qo-, k-). If biological content is about fluids/bathing/figures as the illustrations suggest, these 6 tokens are candidate biological-context-specific vocabulary. The consistent qo-k- patterns are particularly suggestive of a shared morphological/phonological regime in biological.

RECIPES VOCABULARY: 'al' (d=1.68) and 'l' (d=1.60). Both are short tokens. Their concentration in recipes (list-like formulaic text per prior SUFFIX-SUBCLASS analyses) is consistent with formulaic recipe markers. Could be enumerators, counters, measure-words, or list-markers.

HERBAL VOCABULARY: 'daiin' (d=0.73). Relatively weak effect size compared to biological markers. daiin is plausibly the most general-purpose token that nonetheless appears more in herbal. Since Hand A (86% herbal) has daiin as its top token at 1.79% frequency, daiin as herbal-context-marker is now a cross-hand finding.

COSMOLOGICAL/TEXT-ONLY MARKERS: no tokens preferentially concentrate in these sections under the Bonferroni threshold. Both sections have very few folios (3 and 5 respectively) — power is low. Could reflect either real lack of distinctive vocabulary or under-detection due to small samples.

SECTION-AVOIDANCE PATTERN: 'aiin' (d=-1.15 for biological) and 'ar' (d=-1.30 for biological) are strongly DEPLETED in biological. These tokens are common elsewhere but avoid biological. Combined with biological's concentrated markers, this suggests biological uses a distinctive vocabulary regime that partially replaces the default vocabulary with section-specific alternatives.

UNIFYING HAND A AND HAND B: Hand A's 86%-herbal composition explains why Hand A appeared section-independent in H-BV-EXTERNAL-CORRELATION-01. Hand A uses daiin (herbal-marker) heavily because Hand A IS mostly herbal. Under the new reading, both hands show content-coupling; Hand A just happens to mostly contain one content type, masking the effect.

v2 NARRATIVE UPDATE: the manuscript-wide narrative becomes 'Hand A and Hand B both have section-content-linked vocabulary; the hand-level difference in section-correlation patterns reflects sample composition (Hand A mostly herbal), not different underlying encoding regimes.' This is substantially cleaner than the prior 'Hand A is overlay, Hand B is content-coupled' interpretation.

COMPATIBILITY WITH MARKOV-1-FLAT: both hands share the Markov-1-flat character signature AND Hand B (and likely Hand A with enough section diversity) have section-specific vocabulary. This is coherent: a generative process can produce Markov-flat character statistics WHILE having token-level section associations. The text is lexically content-coupled but character-level generator-flat. Possible mechanism: a vocabulary-conditioned generator where section context determines which sub-vocabulary is active, and within each sub-vocabulary the glyph-level transitions are Markov-1.

DECIPHERMENT IMPLICATIONS: 8 concentrated markers with specific section associations are CONCRETE LEXICAL HYPOTHESES. For v2 or subsequent work: hypothesise that shedy/ol/qokain/qokedy/shey/chedy relate to biological content (candidates: water, bath, body, figure, flow, container); al/l relate to recipes (candidates: measure, count, enumerator); daiin relates to herbal (candidates: plant, leaf, root, generic-herb). Any of these hypotheses could be tested against illustration context for specific folios containing these markers.

METHODOLOGY LESSON 11 CANDIDATE: systematic tests across a locked token set (rather than cherry-picked tokens) reveal patterns that single-token tests miss. The top-20-by-frequency locked list prevented post-hoc token selection. 40% concentration rate (8/20) is the structural finding; any single-token test (e.g., just testing qokain) would have found only that one result.
reasoning: H-BV-HAND-B-SECTION-BREAKDOWN-01 established that chedy is a biological-specific marker in Hand B (d=+1.08, biological-sole driver). The natural question: is this one-off or systematic? If Hand B has content-responsive vocabulary, MULTIPLE top-20 tokens should show section-specific concentrations. This hypothesis tests that directly. Decision rule requires >=3 section-concentrated tokens spread across >=2 different sections to confirm the pattern as 'systematic'; 1-2 puts chedy in the 'notable but not widespread' category; 0 makes chedy a genuine outlier and the section effects would then be attributed to diversity rather than specific lexemes. The test locks the top-20 token list by frequency, avoiding post-hoc token selection. Bonferroni at 0.01/100 = 0.0001 controls multiple-testing baseline conservatively.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-17 user-directed systematic test of section-concentration across top-20 Hand B tokens, following H-BV-HAND-B-SECTION-BREAKDOWN-01 chedy-as-biological-marker finding.
